decisionMemorySummary.note: Aggregates Codex recommendation snapshots compared against final human workbench decisions. Missing historical Codex recommendations remain unavailable and are not inferred.

meta.note: Codex-first conservative review. Human QC still required before merge.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Dashboard symbol color/shape ambiguous among front/rear fog and dipped-beam candidates; machine top matches rear-fog while another top candidate maps to front-fog. Visual semantics unclear — defer to human review.
Conservative unresolved decision because evidence was insufficient.

Visual shows a blue sign with a right-pointing arrow (one-way to the right). Candidate matches conflict (top machine q0817 indicates left-one-way while other candidates indicate right one-way). Because of candidate conflict, keep unresolved.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match q0817 indicates a left one-way (D) which conflicts with the visual right-pointing arrow; candidate set inconsistent.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows a straight arrow and a right-turn arrow: meaning 'go straight or turn right'. q0705 corresponds to that meaning. However top machine match (q0848) indicates U-turn — candidate conflict raises risk.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine matcher q0848's correct answer (U-turn) conflicts with the visual semantics (straight + right arrows); q0705 better matches the source image.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Dashboard symbol color/shape ambiguous among front/rear fog and dipped-beam candidates; machine top matches rear-fog while another top candidate maps to front-fog. Visual semantics unclear — defer to human review.

Source asks if overtaking at a level crossing (if no train is seen) is allowed — correct answer is 'No'. q0035 explicitly states overtaking at a railway crossing is prohibited, semantically equivalent. Chosen q0035 for clearer negative formulation.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match q0302 framed the statement positively and labeled it Wrong; q0035 gives an explicit prohibition and maps more directly to the source phrasing.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image of police hand signals present but candidate matches show conflicting options (stop / turn left / wait). Visual signal in provided photo is ambiguous; needs human verification of the pictured gesture sequence.
Red dashboard circle with exclamation matches handbrake/parking-brake indicator. q0534 states it lights when handbrake is pulled up. Source answer 'Yes' corresponds to option A.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match q0571 referenced seatbelt light; selected q0534 explicitly matches handbrake semantics and the visual red circle+exclamation.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Green headlamp-with-rays indicator visually matches dipped-beam (low beam). q0583's correct option is 'low beam'. Source option D corresponds to 'le feu de croisement' (low beam).
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match q0580 labelled front-fog; selected q0583 better matches green headlamp-with-horizontal-rays semantics for low-beam indicator.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows parking-space markings with directional arrows per-spot; source correct answer 'only park in one direction' (C). No high-confidence existing candidate observed; create new question for this exact parking-marking semantics.

Image shows an intersection but no clear prohibitory sign; source statement claims both 'no straight' and 'no left' — candidate q0230/q0639 partial matches (only 'give way' or 'no left'). Ambiguous visual semantics and prompt; cannot safely map.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0230 ('let straight-going vehicle go first') does not match the source claim that straight and left turns are forbidden.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: No visible speed-limit sign in photo; candidate matches show several different speed-limit questions (different option sets). Cannot confirm which master qid, if any, corresponds to the localized options (30/40/50/70).
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0367 uses a different numeric option set (90/100/110/120), so it is not a clear match to the French options.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows three pedals with arrow on the rightmost pedal — in a manual car that is the accelerator. Local options include accelerator (A). Existing top candidate q0585 marks brake as correct, so create new localized question to preserve correct mapping.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0585 labels the brake as correct for its mapping; that contradicts the clear visual semantics (rightmost pedal = accelerator).

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Green rectangular sign with 'G15' likely indicates a highway/route number or beginning of a highway section, but candidate matches disagree on exact meaning and option ordering. Cannot confidently assign existing qid or localized correct letter.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0799 suggests 'highway beginning' but mapping to localized options is unclear and agreement is low.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Conservative unresolved decision because evidence was insufficient.

Triangular warning sign with black zigzag likely warns of a series of bends; candidate matches vary (some propose rockfall). Local options distinguish number of bends — insufficient agreement to map to a master qid or pick a localized correct letter.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0811 indicates falling rocks, which conflicts with the zigzag turns visual; candidate semantics are inconsistent.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Prohibition sign with arrows and red diagonal indicates 'no lane changing' which corresponds to French option B; no clear existing qid match in candidate set.
Image and prompt ask if temporary parking is allowed in non-motorized lane; q0272 has correctRow 'Wrong' (No) matching French option B.

Visual shows a blue sign with a right-pointing arrow (one-way to the right). Candidate matches conflict (top machine q0817 indicates left-one-way while other candidates indicate right one-way). Because of candidate conflict, keep unresolved.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match q0817 indicates a left one-way (D) which conflicts with the visual right-pointing arrow; candidate set inconsistent.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement 'A driver who breaks a provision of the highway code must be punished' is a valid true/false item but no clear existing candidate match; recommend creating new question with answer A (Yes).

Image shows an intersection viewpoint; options concern meaning of the traffic light but visual and candidate matches are ambiguous about which light/state is shown. Cannot confidently map to an existing qid or option.

Image shows a straight arrow and a right-turn arrow: meaning 'go straight or turn right'. q0705 corresponds to that meaning. However top machine match (q0848) indicates U-turn — candidate conflict raises risk.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine matcher q0848's correct answer (U-turn) conflicts with the visual semantics (straight + right arrows); q0705 better matches the source image.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source asks if instrument shows fuel level within warning (needle near '0' in embedded image). No clear existing qid about low-fuel gauge among top candidates; create new question. Visual evidence supports answer 'Yes'.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine candidates refer to unrelated dashboard indicators (high beam, seatbelt, other lamps) — none specifically match a low-fuel gauge.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source asks meaning of continuous white lines on each side of the road; the most consistent interpretation is prohibition to cross (D). No clear matching candidate found in shortlist.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine candidates are about driver behaviors/indicators and do not address side continuous white lines; no clear existing qid match.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source statement ≃ machine q0003 prompt; both assert 'frequently change lanes on highway' is false. Language and intent match; return source letter for 'No' (B).

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement denies overtaking on a dangerous curve even if traffic is light. Top candidate q0067 has same semantic (wrong to permit overtaking); map French 'No' to option B.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source states a hit-and-run driver cannot apply for a new license; top candidate q0223 has the same legal assertion. Map 'Yes' to French option A.

Indicator shown is a green headlight icon; source claims it means high-beam. Standard semantics: high-beam indicator is blue, not green — statement is false. Top candidate q0528 asserts same. Map 'No' to French option B.

Blue sign with pedestrian pictogram denotes pedestrian area/passage reserved for pedestrians. Candidate q0890 'pedestrian zone' matches; local option D ('passage réservé aux piétons') corresponds to that meaning.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement matches q0059: reduce speed and drive on the right in a sharp/dangerous curve; French option A = Yes matches qid's 'Right/True'.

Triangular warning sign with traffic-light symbol indicates 'pay attention to traffic lights' / 'attention aux feux de signalisation' which maps to q0765; French option B corresponds to that meaning.

Visual: triangular warning sign showing car entering a tunnel. Source option C ('allumer ses feux dans le tunnel') matches master q0814 ('turn on headlights in the tunnel'). Strong image + option semantic match.

Image shows a straight arrow and a right-turn arrow: meaning 'go straight or turn right'. q0705 corresponds to that meaning. However top machine match (q0848) indicates U-turn — candidate conflict raises risk.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source statement matches q0087 text about stopping, observing and choosing a solid section when encountering muddy road. Machine match also indicates 'Right'.
Source advises controlling speed in heavy/torrential rain to avoid hydroplaning; matches q0033 which is 'Right'.

Source asks if instrument shows fuel level within warning (needle near '0' in embedded image). No clear existing qid about low-fuel gauge among top candidates; create new question. Visual evidence supports answer 'Yes'.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source statement (vehicle may enter provided speed >60 km/h on access lane) corresponds to q0040 which states this is incorrect (Wrong). Machine matched q0040 as top candidate.
Source asks what to do at an accident requiring assistance: securing the scene and contacting police matches q0147 option C. Top candidate also lists C as correct though overall match score is low.
Source: before mountain curve with no oncoming vehicles, reduce speed, honk and keep right — matches q0130 option A. Candidate q0130 is top match despite low overall scores.
Source: encountering a traffic jam, driver should slow down and wait in queue; matches q0182 option C. Top candidate supports this.

Source asks what to do when meeting at an improper place to pass; meaning matches q0170 (reduce speed / stop and yield). French source correct choice is 'ralentir ou s'arrêter et laisser passer' (option B) which maps to master correct behavior.

No image provided and options are generic sign-type categories. Candidate matches point to directional/prohibitory/indicative/warning but without the sign visual it's ambiguous — cannot safely map to an existing qid.

Specific numeric question about minibus safety distance (<100 km/h → at least 50 m). No existing candidate precisely covers 'minibus <100 km/h' distance; recommend creating new localized item. Source indicates option A (50 m).

Image shows a yellow triangular warning sign with a zigzag line (series of bends), but candidate qids are mixed (various curve/warning signs). Unclear which exact master qid corresponds to the pictured variant (two bends vs more than two); prefer human review.
Statement matches q0305: vehicles on emergency missions (fire, ambulance, tow) must be yielded to. Source options Yes/No — correct = Yes (French option A).
Source claims after a tire blowout the driver should refrain from using the foot brake before controlling speed — matches q0023 (true). French option A (Yes) corresponds to correct.
Source: police may suspend/detain license if driver lent vehicle to person whose license was revoked — aligns with q0249 (detain license). French option A (Yes) is the true statement.
Matches q0121: if you miss an exit on the highway you should continue ahead and take the next exit. French option B corresponds to master correct option.

Question about whether an end-of-life vehicle, after full repair, may be used is a regulatory/legal item not matched by top candidates. The likely correct answer is 'No' (French option B), but existing qids don't represent this exact rule — create new localized question and human-verify.

Statement that a vehicle deemed unusable under national regulation cannot be registered is a specific legal assertion not covered by candidate items. Recommend creating a new question; source answer appears to be Yes (French option A).
